strategies.name: RSI(21) Divergence Reversal (Bearish/Bullish with S/R Confirmation)
strategies.description: A mean reversion/reversal strategy using RSI set to 21 periods (not standard 14) combined with price action confirmation. Identifies divergence between price and RSI (price making higher highs while RSI makes lower highs for bearish, inverse for bullish), then waits for price to break through a support/resistance zone AND RSI to cross the 50% level before entering. Targets previous order blocks. The speaker explicitly warns against trading on divergence alone — confirmation via S/R breakout and RSI 50 cross is mandatory.

entry_signals.condition: Bearish divergence: price makes higher highs while RSI(21) makes lower highs. Bullish divergence: price makes lower lows while RSI(21) makes higher lows.
entry_signals.value: divergence detected between price swing extremes and RSI swing extremes

entry_signals.condition: Price breaks below support zone (bearish divergence) OR breaks above resistance zone (bullish divergence). Horizontal zones drawn from recent swing highs and swing lows.

parameters.break_confirmation: candle close beyond the zone (not just a wick)

entry_signals.condition: RSI(21) breaks below 50 (bearish confirmation) OR breaks above 50 (bullish confirmation). This confirms the regime change.

exit_rules.condition: Price reaches the previous order block — the origin zone of the prior significant move in the opposite direction

parameters.target_definition: origin candle/zone of the last significant directional move
parameters.minimum_rr: not explicitly stated, but examples show 2:1+

exit_rules.condition: Price moves back beyond the broken S/R zone, invalidating the breakdown/breakout
exit_rules.value: slightly beyond the broken S/R zone

parameters.bearish_stop: slightly above the broken support zone
parameters.bullish_stop: slightly below the broken resistance zone

additional_rules: NEVER enter on divergence alone — always require S/R break + RSI 50 cross
NEVER buy just because RSI is oversold — market can stay oversold and keep dropping
Overbought/oversold zones are NOT entry signals by themselves

filters.rsi_50_zone: above 50 = bullish regime, below 50 = bearish regime — key dividing line
filters.confirmation_required: divergence + S/R break + RSI 50 cross — all three required
filters.overbought_oversold_warning: do NOT use overbought/oversold as entry signals

missing_elements: Specific timeframe recommendation (15-min demonstrated but 'any' claimed)
Handling multiple divergence signals in quick succession
assumptions: Standard divergence definition (comparing swing extremes of price vs RSI)
Order blocks = origin candle/zone of significant directional move
S/R zones from recent swing highs and lows
Entry at market on confirmed breakdown/breakout
source_quotes: I use the RSI on a period of 21, not the standard 14
The 50% zone is the most important level
Never buy just because RSI is oversold
Divergence is a signal, NOT a trigger — requires confirmation

strategy_names: RSI(21) Divergence Reversal (Bearish/Bullish with S/R Confirmation)
summary.extraction_notes: Clear conceptual framework with chart examples but no backtest data or performance statistics. RSI period of 21 and emphasis on 50% zone are the key differentiators from standard RSI strategies. Strategy 1 of 2 from this video.
summary.video_title: The Only 2 RSI Trading Strategies that REALLY Work
summary.primary_trading_style: Mean reversion / reversal with divergence + S/R confirmation
summary.automation_feasibility: MEDIUM — RSI(21) calculation is trivial. Divergence detection between price swing points and RSI swing points is moderately complex but well-defined algorithmically. Support/resistance zone detection from swing points is standard. The main challenge is defining order block targets programmatically. RSI 50 cross is a simple condition.
summary.key_insight: The 50% RSI level is the critical dividing line between bullish and bearish regimes — more important than overbought/oversold zones. Combined with the non-standard 21-period setting, this creates a slower, more reliable divergence filter than standard RSI(14).
